passing_hypotheses.title: HDAC3-NCoR Eraser Depletion by ECM Stiffness Creates Enhancer Stabilization Independent of Writer Activation
passing_hypotheses.mechanism_summary: ECM stiffness downregulates HDAC3 protein, disabling the NCoR/SMRT corepressor complex that normally decommissions enhancers by removing H3K27ac. Without HDAC3 eraser activity, existing H3K27ac at active enhancers is stabilized — a mechanism orthogonal to all writer-activation models (YAP-EP300) that dominate the field. Predicts that RGFP966 (HDAC3 inhibitor) on soft ECM phenocopies a subset of stiff-ECM enhancers, while A-485 (EP300 inhibitor) on stiff ECM eliminates writer-activated but NOT eraser-stabilized enhancers.
passing_hypotheses.key_prediction: RGFP966 on soft ECM phenocopies eraser-stabilized subset; A-485 on stiff ECM spares eraser-stabilized H3K27ac; HDAC3 AAV rescue collapses them
passing_hypotheses.test_protocol_summary: Chondrocytes/ATDC5 on PDMS (1/50 kPa, 48h). HDAC3 WB + ChIP-seq. H3K27ac ChIP-seq + ATAC-seq. Triple perturbation: RGFP966, A-485, HDAC3 AAV.

passing_hypotheses.title: H3K27ac as 6-12h Temporal Window for TET2-Mediated CpG Demethylation -> DNA Methylation Mechanical Memory
passing_hypotheses.mechanism_summary: ECM stiffness-induced H3K27ac creates a transient 6-12h window during which TET2 is recruited to mechano-responsive enhancers, oxidizing 5mC to 5hmC. Base excision repair completes CpG demethylation within 24-72h. The demethylated CpGs persist as stable mechanical memory (days-weeks) long after H3K27ac decays. Cells previously exposed to stiffness show faster H3K27ac re-induction upon re-stiffening (epigenetic priming), and siTET2 in the first cycle abolishes this priming effect.
passing_hypotheses.key_prediction: RRBS shows CpG demethylation at mechano-enhancer shores; 5hmC DIP-seq enrichment at 12h; TET2 CUT&RUN enrichment at stiffness-gained peaks; siTET2 abolishes demethylation; primed cells show >1.5-fold faster re-induction
passing_hypotheses.test_protocol_summary: hMSCs on PA hydrogels. CUT&Tag + TET2 CUT&RUN + 5hmC DIP-seq + RRBS. siTET1/siTET2. A-485 timing. Cycling experiment (50->1->50 kPa).
passing_hypotheses.key_risk: TET2 at stiffness-gained enhancers is entirely parametric

passing_hypotheses.title: Sequential Two-Phase Bivalent Enhancer Activation: YAP-EP300 Phase 1 -> UTX/MLL3-COMPASS Phase 2
passing_hypotheses.mechanism_summary: ECM stiffness triggers a two-phase enhancer activation cascade. Phase 1 (0-4h): YAP-TEAD recruits EP300 to non-bivalent enhancers (CTGF/CYR61). Phase 2 (12-24h): EP300-MLL3/4-COMPASS co-occupancy at Phase 1 sites enables feedforward UTX(KDM6A) recruitment to adjacent bivalent enhancers (SNAI1/RUNX2 distal), which demethylates H3K27me3. The 8-14h temporal gap between phases is the central falsifiable prediction, tested by temporal A-485 application and four-arm siRNA paralog disambiguation (siKDM6A vs siKDM6B).
passing_hypotheses.key_prediction: Temporal gap >=8h between Phase 1 and Phase 2 H3K27ac peaks; siKDM6A reduces bivalent enhancers >50%, siKDM6B <20%; A-485 at 8h spares Phase 1 but blocks Phase 2
passing_hypotheses.test_protocol_summary: hMSCs on PA hydrogels. CUT&Tag H3K27ac+H3K27me3 time-course. KDM6A CUT&RUN. Four-arm siRNA. A-485 at 0h vs 8h. re-ChIP at SNAI1.
passing_hypotheses.key_risk: UTX-COMPASS may be recruited independently via MLL3/4 PHD domain

passing_hypotheses.title: Integrin Force-Induced H3K9me3 Demethylation at Nuclear Interior Enhancers Creates Competence Windows
passing_hypotheses.mechanism_summary: ECM stiffness transmits mechanical force through integrins and the LINC complex to the nuclear interior, where it induces H3K9me3 demethylation at a subset of enhancers (H3K4me1+/H3K9me3+). This creates 'competence windows' — enhancers that become accessible (ATAC+) but are not yet active (no H3K27ac). Subsequent YAP-EP300 signaling then activates these competent enhancers. The two-step model predicts that chaetocin on soft ECM creates accessible-but-inactive enhancers, while chaetocin + YAP(S127A) produces full activation.
passing_hypotheses.key_prediction: H3K9me3 loss at 1-4h BEFORE H3K27ac gain at 6-24h at nuclear interior enhancers; chaetocin creates ATAC+ but H3K27ac- state; ML324 blocks competence window
passing_hypotheses.test_protocol_summary: hMSCs on PA hydrogels. CUT&Tag (H3K9me3+H3K27ac+H3K4me1) + ATAC-seq + DamID-seq. Chaetocin +/- YAP(S127A). ML324. Sun 2020 magnetic bead replication.
passing_hypotheses.key_risk: Sun 2020 used cyclic force, not static ECM stiffness; KDM4A identity speculative; 50% groundedness borderline

passing_hypotheses.title: Dual YAP-TEAD + MRTF-SRF Programs in CTCF-Permitted Loop Domains
passing_hypotheses.mechanism_summary: ECM stiffness simultaneously activates two independent H3K27ac programs: YAP-TEAD (at CTGF/CYR61 enhancers) and MRTF-SRF (at ACTA2/VCL enhancers). Each program increases enhancer-promoter contact frequency within pre-established CTCF-anchored loop domains via NIPBL-cohesin extrusion. H3K27ac HiChIP reveals two non-overlapping contact networks (<20% shared genes) that are independently suppressible by verteporfin (YAP program) vs C3-transferase (MRTF program).
passing_hypotheses.key_prediction: Two non-overlapping HiChIP contact networks (>3-fold enrichment each); >70% CTCF-anchored; verteporfin suppresses Network A not B; C3-transferase suppresses Network B not A
passing_hypotheses.test_protocol_summary: MCF10A on PA hydrogels. H3K27ac HiChIP + ChIP-seq + CTCF ChIP-seq + NIPBL CUT&RUN. Verteporfin, C3-transferase, siNIPBL, JQ1. 4C-seq validation.
passing_hypotheses.key_risk: Limited mechanistic novelty; mechanism is relatively obvious extension of known biology
